reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND in-page in headless Chrome — real single-stage multi-person keypoints grounded on bodies; pose->CLIP multi-model verified; desktop+mobile layout checked; page-load conformance clean), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (raw ONNX Runtime Web 1.21.0, WASM EP, fresh profile). Standalone: Xenova/yolov8n-pose onnx/model.onnx (fp32, input 'images' [1,3,640,640] -> output 'output0' [1,56,8400]) on a single-athlete photo returned person conf 0.919 with 17 grounded COCO keypoints (nose/eyes/ears/shoulders/elbows/wrists/hips/knees/ankles all on the right body parts). In-page (CDP driver, fresh profile -> Download ~13 MB -> auto-init ready -> Detect): the basics page found 4 people on the players photo with grounded keypoints (person #1 nose 519,117 conf 0.986), 0 console errors. NOT faked.
rubric.notes: All inference AND the letterbox preprocessing run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the objectness grid buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page. transformers.js registers no YOLO class, so the ONNX graph runs directly via an isolated per-worker onnxruntime-web pin (precedent: yolov10-detection).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a [1,56,8400] head decoded to per-person {box, conf, 17x(x,y,visibility)}. The worker filters by confidence, runs Non-Max-Suppression, and maps boxes+keypoints from 640-letterbox space back to source pixels; the page draws the 17-point COCO skeleton on each person. Semantic sanity confirmed: joints land on actual body parts and confidences are high on visible joints (0.38-0.99).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT approach from the built ViTPose (top-down heatmap transformer needing a person box per crop) and MediaPipe BlazePose: YOLOv8-pose is single-stage and multi-person — one pass poses everyone (4 players verified).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/yolov8n-pose, linked), the task (keypoint-detection), the architecture ([1,56,8400] single-stage head), quantisation (fp32, onnx/model.onnx), runtime (onnxruntime-web — raw ORT because no transformers.js YOLO class exists), backend (WASM), size (~13 MB), and license (AGPL-3.0, same family as the built yolov10/yolo11 demos).
rubric.notes: Inference is off the main thread; the objectness grid is exposed for 'see inside'.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/yolov8n-pose', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:13}); the worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage under the '/Xenova/yolov8n-pose/' path so lib/model-cache.js scans it -> auto-init on a returning visit, honest Download on first visit, per-model clear-cache once ready.
rubric.notes: A fresh profile correctly showed Download (~13 MB) then auto-initialised to ready with no extra Load click (verified in the CDP driver).

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/detecting status is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Detecting.../Found N person(s) status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Pose estimation failed: ...').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds. The optional webcam mode (wild) is user-initiated with explicit Stop + track cleanup, and falls back to photos if the camera is unavailable.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: file drop/picker (role=button, Enter/Space), sample thumbnails (role=button, keyboard), Detect button, confidence slider, Draw-boxes checkbox (index), person selector (basics/practical), limb/joint sliders + stage/colour/glow controls (wild), activity-labels text input + Pose&label (multi-model). The wild page adds user-initiated webcam with Stop.
rubric.notes: Ladder: basics (raw keypoints table per person), practical (elbow/knee/back joint angles + plain-language feedback), wild (stick-figure puppet studio + optional live webcam), multi-model (pose->CLIP activity labelling).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real ONNX graph; every keypoint, box, angle, objectness-grid cell, and CLIP label is derived from real on-device runs. Multi-model verified: pose->CLIP labelled 4 players 'playing football' (77-84%) from real per-person crops fed to CLIP zero-shot.
rubric.notes: The joint-angle feedback (practical) is honest geometry on the model's real keypoints, clearly labelled as rule-based, not a second model.

rubric.evidence: All buttons have accessible names (conformance buttons-have-names pass); sample images carry alt text (images-have-alt pass); the stage canvas carries an aria-label describing the drawn skeletons; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); sliders/selects are native labelled controls. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: The objectness heatmap and per-person tables convey the same information as text, so the canvas visual is not the only channel.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + logical properties; the stage canvas is inline-size:100% with max-block-size:64vh; readouts and control rows wrap; sample thumbs are 76x56. Measured 0px horizontal overflow at desktop (1280x800) and narrow mobile (360x740, DPR3) across index + all 4 ladder pages, light+dark.
rubric.notes: Mobile download cost (~13 MB pose; ~340 MB extra for the CLIP multi-model page) surfaced on the page. Tap targets meet ~44px on primary controls.

rubric.evidence: All inference + the letterbox preprocessing run off the main thread in a Web Worker; ImageBitmaps are transferred in and the objectness grid buffer transferred out (not cloned). The skeleton draw is a bounded set of lines/arcs on a canvas (not a per-pixel dense composite), redrawn only on run/slider change. INP stays low — inference never blocks the interface.
rubric.notes: The wild webcam loop guards against overlap (busy flag) and runs one frame at a time via requestAnimationFrame.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, heavy work + dense pre/post off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all ONNX inference AND the letterbox preprocessing run in a dedicated module Web Worker; ImageBitmaps are transferred in, the objectness grid buffer transferred out; the skeleton draw is a bounded lines/arcs pass, not a per-pixel composite.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([gridConf.buffer]); UI responsive during detection; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and canvas stage without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — control rows/readouts flex-wrap; the stage canvas is inline-size:100% with max-block-size:64vh; the multi-model results grid uses auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow across index + 4 ladder pages, light+dark

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading/detecting/error states
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; drop/sample controls are role=button with Enter/Space handlers; every button/slider/select is labelled.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas-rendered skeleton content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content and duplicate it in text.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the stage canvas has an aria-label describing the drawn skeletons; the per-person keypoint/angle/activity tables convey the same data as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text tables duplicate the visual

openQuestions: The multi-model page loads CLIP (~340 MB) on top of the ~13 MB pose model — worth a q4 CLIP option or a lazy 'label' step to keep the mobile download down.
A quantised (q8/fp16) yolov8n-pose export would cut the ~13 MB fp32 download further and might speed WASM inference — watch for an onnx-community pose export with a validated quantised head.
Should the wild webcam loop cap resolution on mobile to protect INP on lower-end devices?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a YOLOv8-pose 'segment/track' wild demo that counts reps or tracks a single person across a short webcam clip (temporal smoothing on the keypoints).
Offer a q8/fp16 dtype toggle if a validated quantised yolov8n-pose ONNX head becomes available (smaller mobile download).

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for yolov8-pose (Xenova/yolov8n-pose, keypoint-detection, fp32, ~13 MB — a SINGLE-STAGE detector+pose model, distinct from the built top-down ViTPose and MediaPipe BlazePose). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome via raw ONNX Runtime Web (transformers.js has no YOLO class): standalone the [1,56,8400] head decoded (with NMS) to 17 grounded COCO keypoints at person conf 0.919; in-page the basics demo found 4 people on the players photo with grounded keypoints and 0 console errors, and the multi-model pose->CLIP chain labelled all 4 players 'playing football' (77-84%) from real per-person crops. Off-main-thread worker (letterbox + inference + transferred objectness grid). Ladder: basics (raw keypoints), practical (joint angles + feedback), wild (stick-figure puppet studio + optional user-initiated webcam), multi-model (pose->CLIP action labelling). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. AGPL-3.0 (same YOLO family/license as the built yolov10/yolo11 detection demos), recorded in at-a-glance.
